beats.title: Recap and Raina's Plea
beats.boundaryReason: The opening stays in one continuous tavern setup scene: the DM recaps Hamri's recent history, brings the party together at the Leviathan, and has Raina explain the dreams, the song fragment, and the hidden cave she wants found.
dateEvidence: at u0090 the DM explicitly states, 'It is now May 26, 1749'
at u0098 the session proper begins 'in the evening of a pleasantly warm spring day'
nothing before u0246 suggests sleep, travel to another day, or a break from that opening evening meeting

beats.title: Questions Around the Leviathan
beats.boundaryReason: After hearing Raina out, the party stays in Hamri gathering local context: they discuss the dream, consult Yaz about Guzu and the shrine, summon Ovina for older town memory, and settle on heading for the cliffside cave.
dateEvidence: this beat follows directly from the tavern briefing with no rest or date transition
u0539 says they are 'approaching dinner time now,' keeping the whole inquiry in the same evening stretch
the scene ends when the group commits to going out immediately rather than waiting for another day

beats.title: Finding the Buried Shrine
beats.boundaryReason: The next phase is the next day's search itself: the party takes Ryu's boat to the North Cliff, uses scouting and local memory to find the right collapsed sea cave, and confirms they have reached the buried shrine of Guzu.
dateEvidence: u0625 explicitly says, 'So the next morning'
u0626-u0629 describe a bright, calm day after the previous evening's storm
u0634-u0637 place the party on Ryu's boat heading out after breakfast rather than continuing the May 26 tavern scene

beats.title: Into the Sea Cave
beats.boundaryReason: Once they dive through the blasted opening, the whole beat is about first contact inside the cave: scouting the beach, spotting the water creatures, approaching the ruined temple, and browbeating the trapped minions into cooperating and leading them inward.
dateEvidence: this beat begins immediately after the morning scouting run finds the collapsed entrance
nothing between beats 003 and 004 indicates a rest or date change
the dive, beach approach, and first exchange with the shrine creatures all continue the same morning cave expedition

beats.title: Secrets of Guzu's Shrine
beats.boundaryReason: Inside the temple the party investigates the statue, inscriptions, offerings, and chapel, discovers the prayer that opens the hidden passage, and reveals the trapped water boss holding the song fragment behind the vanished stone door.
dateEvidence: the shrine exploration follows directly from the morning cave approach with no pause or return to town
the longer search of the chapel, books, offerings, and hidden door reads as later in the same outing
there is no date rollover before the inner chamber reveal, only continued investigation deeper in the shrine

beats.title: A Bad Deal Offered
beats.boundaryReason: Before initiative starts, the party tries to bluff and bargain with the trapped elemental, learns how the blue light and shard confinement work, debates whether to let him go, and finally shifts from talk to a plan to bottle him up and fight in the temple.
dateEvidence: this scene begins immediately on the inner chamber reveal, before anyone rolls initiative
the entire exchange is one continuous negotiation and tactical debate in the same midday shrine scene
u1613 is the first actual initiative call, so everything before it remains part of that uninterrupted confrontation

beats.title: Jet of Water
beats.boundaryReason: Initiative begins with the party's attempted bottleneck plan turning into open combat: the boss lashes out with the first massive water jet, the front line scrambles to contain the doorway, and the opening exchange ends with Geo dropping and the boss still entrenched.
dateEvidence: u1613 explicitly calls for initiative
the action from the first water jet through Geo's collapse is one continuous opening combat phase with no reset
this is still the same May 27 shrine expedition, with the fight now occupying the later part of the day

beats.title: Blue Light Advantage
beats.boundaryReason: The middle of the fight turns in the party's favor: Nerissa's spell and the shrine's blue glow drive the boss back, the minions flood the stairs, and focused blows from the party leave the trapped elemental bloodied and calling for help.
dateEvidence: this beat begins mid-initiative directly after Geo falls
the transcript presents the blue light's interference and the boss's bloodied state as one continuous combat phase
there is no rest, withdrawal, or scene change before u2171 marks the boss reeling under Wazir's hits later that same day

beats.title: Mist and Running Water
beats.boundaryReason: With the boss desperate, the battle shifts again: scalding water erupts near Ryu, the minions swarm and start breaking, the elemental turns to mist to slip through the wall, and the party fights to keep control of the beach-temple choke point.
dateEvidence: the beat starts as the bloodied boss's allies and ranged attacks change the fight's shape
the mist-form escape attempt and the ongoing minion melee are part of the same uninterrupted initiative sequence
the phase ends only when the boss slips through the wall and the opportunity attacks resolve during the ongoing afternoon battle

beats.title: The Song Fragment Returns
beats.boundaryReason: The final stretch covers the fight's cleanup and immediate aftermath: the remaining creatures flee or are cut down, the boss dies with a threat in the wind, Nerissa hears the crystal's grateful song, the party loots what little remains, and they return the fragment to Raina before the epilogue in Hamri.
dateEvidence: this beat begins immediately after the boss's wall-escape attempt resolves
u2690-u2691 compresses the last enemy cleanup into the same combat aftermath rather than a new scene on another day
u2739 explicitly says, 'That afternoon, you return to the Leviathan Inn'
the handoff to Raina and the Hamri epilogue therefore remain on May 27, not the evening of May 26
